qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Unbounded PBES2 KDF parameters in PKCS#8 parsing allow attacker-triggered resource exhaustion
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: This finding concerns PBES2 KDF cost exhaustion in pkcs8.ParsePrivateKey and related PKCS#8 decryption helpers; the advisory concerns SM9 Decrypt/DecryptASN1/UnwrapKey accepting the point at infinity and enabling ciphertext forgery, so the affected code and root cause differ.

target_advisory_judgement.reason: None of the qualifying findings identify the advisory's SM9 ciphertext-forgery root cause: failure to reject infinity-point C1 in the shared sm9.UnwrapKey decryption path.
evidence: The advisory describes an integrity flaw in SM9 decryption and decapsulation paths (sm9.Decrypt, sm9.DecryptASN1, sm9.UnwrapKey) where C1 is only checked for successful deserialization and on-curve status, not rejected when it is the point at infinity.

The pkcs8_kdf_resource_exhaustion finding targets PBES2 KDF parameter handling in pkcs8.ParsePrivateKey and pkcs.PBES2Params.Decrypt, causing parser resource exhaustion before decryption failure rather than SM9 ciphertext forgery.
